mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS is falling or remains at an exceptionally low level, the discount-rate channel supports equity valuation expansion and lifts S&P 500 returns over the next month. The signal is strongest when inflation is not accelerating faster than policy easing.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day SPX forward returns are not positive on average during falling-FEDFUNDS episodes, or if the effect disappears when CPIAUCSL inflation is stable to decelerating; also reject if triggered samples produce negative returns in more than 55% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in UNRATE above its recent level signals weakening household income expectations and deteriorating risk appetite, which pushes S&P 500 returns lower over the next month. The signal should be especially negative when payroll growth is also soft.
failure_condition: Reject if SPX 20-day returns are positive in a majority of episodes where UNRATE rises more than 0.2pp over 3 months, or if the signal is neutral when PAYEMS growth remains positive.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in RSAFS indicates stronger nominal consumption demand and revenue tailwinds for large-cap firms, which should translate into positive S&P 500 returns over a one-week to one-month horizon. The effect weakens when retail sales are rising only because of price inflation rather than real demand.
failure_condition: Reject if positive RSAFS momentum fails to coincide with positive 5-day or 20-day SPX returns, or if the relationship vanishes when CPIAUCSL is accelerating faster than RSAFS.

mechanism_narrative: An upturn in INDPRO reflects firmer industrial activity and a rebound in cyclical earnings expectations, which should raise S&P 500 returns over the following quarter. The signal is strongest when housing activity is also stable or improving.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO rises above its 3-month moving average
failure_condition: Reject if quarters with INDPRO above its 3-month moving average do not show positive 60-day SPX returns on average, or if the effect is absent when HOUST is rising.

mechanism_narrative: Weak HOUST signals a cooling housing cycle that feeds into softer construction, consumer durables demand, and broader cyclically sensitive earnings, which should pressure S&P 500 returns over the next month. The mechanism fails when housing weakness is offset by strong labor-market momentum.
failure_condition: Reject if SPX 20-day returns are not negative after HOUST drops more than 5% from its 3-month average, or if PAYEMS growth neutralizes the sign in most triggered cases.

mechanism_narrative: A low and stable FEDFUNDS rate supports equity multiples by keeping financing costs and discount rates subdued, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter. The effect is strongest when inflation remains contained.
failure_condition: Reject if SPX 60-day returns are not positive when FEDFUNDS is below 0.5%, or if the effect disappears during contained-inflation periods and turns negative in more than half of observations.

mechanism_narrative: Stable or declining payroll growth together with a flat unemployment rate indicates a mature expansion in which labor gains no longer surprise positively, reducing upside for risk assets over the next week. This should create a mild negative short-horizon drift in S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: Reject if 5-day SPX returns are positive on average when PAYEMS growth is flat and UNRATE is unchanged, or if the sign reverses when labor data are stable.

mechanism_narrative: When GDP growth and industrial production move together, the market receives a joint confirmation of real activity that should support S&P 500 returns over the next quarter. The signal is not present if GDP strengthens while INDPRO weakens, because that pattern implies mixed growth quality.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 and INDPRO both rise above their prior-period levels
failure_condition: Reject if simultaneous increases in GDPC1 and INDPRO do not precede positive 60-day SPX returns, or if the relationship fails whenever one series rises without the other.
